resources.string: ContentReg — reel & content blocking. Grant the permissions below to activate.

Lets ContentReg see which app is in the foreground, which tab is open, and the on-screen text — used to block short-video (Reels/Shorts) feeds and cover explicit content with a block screen. All processing happens on your device; screen contents are never sent anywhere.
Accessibility: OFF — tap below to enable
Overlay permission: OFF — needed to block
Press Back to keep using the app.
When on, the Reels/Shorts tab in these apps is blocked; other tabs work normally. TikTok is all short video, so it is blocked whole-app.
Grant these so the app can sense, block, and filter. You can change them anytime in system settings.
Detects the foreground app and which tab is open (e.g. a Reels/Shorts feed), and reads on-screen text.
Draws the block screen over the Reels/Shorts tab (and blocked pages).
Blocks explicit domains on-device. No traffic leaves your phone.
Makes the app harder to delete: Android blocks uninstalling until you turn it off. Friction, not a lock — Safe Mode or a factory reset still work.
While active, blocks the app from being uninstalled until you turn it off here — so a moment of weakness can\'t undo your setup.

Turn this on to make ContentReg harder to remove. While active, Android won\'t let the app be uninstalled until you deactivate it — here in the app or in Settings. You can turn it off at any time. This does not lock your phone or read anything.
Turning this off removes ContentReg\'s uninstall protection, so the app can then be deleted. Are you sure?
On — the app can\'t be uninstalled until you turn this off.
Off — the app can be uninstalled normally.
Pin a home-screen shortcut with a photo you choose and your own label — it opens the app. (Stock Android can\'t change the built-in app icon to a photo; this is the supported alternative.)
Shortcut request sent — confirm it on your home screen.
Your launcher doesn\'t support pinning shortcuts.
Couldn\'t read that image. Try another.
Before you start: how ContentReg works

ContentReg helps you cut back on doomscrolling and avoid explicit content. To do that, it watches what happens on your screen. Please read this before turning anything on.

WHAT IT ACCESSES
• The app currently in the foreground and which tab is open (e.g. a Reels/Shorts feed).
• The text and links visible on your screen, including your browser's address bar.
• The domain names (e.g. example.com) your device looks up while the URL filter is on.

WHY
• To detect when a short-video (Reels/Shorts) feed is open and block that tab.
• To detect explicit on-screen text and cover it with a block screen.
• To block domains you choose to filter.

WHERE YOUR DATA GOES
• All of this is analysed on your device. The text on your screen and your browsing history are never uploaded, shared, or sent to us or any server.
• One honest exception: to filter domains, the app runs a local VPN that inspects DNS lookups. Allowed lookups are forwarded to a DNS resolver — your device's normal resolver, or a public one such as 8.8.8.8 — exactly as they would be without this app. That is ordinary internet networking, not us collecting your history, and the app keeps no remote log of it.
• To detect a Reels/Shorts feed, the app may let those apps use the network normally (it isn't kept offline). We never send your screen contents, the text on it, or your activity anywhere — nothing about you leaves your device.

CRASH LOGS
• If the app crashes, the details are written to a log stored only on your device. Nothing is sent automatically. You can view it in the Logs screen and share it yourself if you choose to report a problem.

DIGITAL DETOX
• You can choose to lock yourself into a few selected apps for a set time. This is opt-in and you set it up yourself. To unlock before the timer ends, you agree to donate to a charity — this is on the honour system and is stated clearly before you start.

YOU'RE IN CONTROL
• Accessibility, Display-over-other-apps and VPN are granted by you and can be turned off at any time — in Android Settings or here in the app. Turning one off disables that feature.

By tapping "I understand & agree", you consent to the on-device monitoring described above.
Lock yourself to a few chosen apps for a set time. Once it starts, the only way out early is a charity donation.

Pick how long, choose the apps you\'re still allowed to open, then type your signature to begin. Everything else will be blocked. To unlock before the timer ends, you\'ll have to donate to charity — there is no other way out.
Tick the apps you\'ll still be allowed to open during the detox. This app stays available automatically.
Create your signature (you\'ll re-type this to unlock early)
Your signature is a private phrase used only as a speed-bump — it\'s stored on your device and guards nothing else.
Reminder: the ONLY way to unlock before the timer ends is to donate to charity. Are you sure?
This app is blocked during your detox.
You didn\'t allow any extra apps.

You chose to lock yourself in, and the timer isn\'t up yet. As agreed when you started, the only way to unlock early is to donate to a charity.\n\nOpen one of the charities below, make a donation, then come back and type your signature to unlock. We can\'t verify the donation — this runs on the honour system, the same promise you made to yourself.
If the app ever crashes, the details are recorded here on your device. Nothing is sent anywhere automatically — tap Share to send a log if you want to report a problem.
No crashes recorded. Nothing to show yet.
No signature set yet — you\'ll create one when you start your first detox, or set it here.
Your signature is locked while a detox is running — that\'s what stops it being used to skip the charity unlock. You can change it once the detox ends.

⚠ Private DNS is on, so the URL filter can\'t inspect these lookups and some sites may slip past it. Reel and on-screen-text blocking still work. To let the URL filter work, set Private DNS to Off in Android Settings → Network → Private DNS.
